commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0002eb0770e80c257ae57905ea60487c24a9cd6a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `session.maintenance.modelRunPruneAfter` with pressure-gated stale gateway model-run probe cleanup, CLI/reporting output, tests, and docs.

PR surface: Source +228, Tests +439, Docs +22. Total +689 across 30 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main creates `agent:<agent>:explicit:model-run-<uuid>` gateway probe sessions and only has global prune/cap cleanup, while the linked issue includes production store counts showing those rows crowding the cap.
commands.review_followup: - There is a narrow mechanical blocker: preserve SDK source compatibility for the new maintenance config fields before normal maintainer review.

target.title: fix(sessions): prune stale gateway model-run sessions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds `session.maintenance.modelRunPruneAfter` to clean up stale gateway model-run probe sessions (`agent:*:explicit:model-run-<uuid>`) that otherwise accumulate until they hit the global `maxEntries` cap and evict real sessions (#88632).

The default is **pressure-gated** rather than eager, so upgrades do not silently delete old probe-shaped rows from otherwise-healthy stores:

- `modelRunPruneAfter` **unset** → 24h retention, but model-run pruning only runs **when session-entry cap pressure is reached** (before the global stale-prune and `maxEntries` cap). This targets #88632's actual failure mode — probes contributing to cap pressure and displacing real sessions — without touching healthy stores.
- explicit duration/number (e.g. `"24h"`, `"48h"`) → opt into **unconditional** age-based cleanup whenever maintenance runs.
- `false` → disabled.

Pruning is gated consistently across the three maintenance paths (runtime write, load-time enforcement, `sessions cleanup`). For forced maintenance (`sessions cleanup` / explicit override) the cap applies immediately, so model-run pruning is aligned to the same cap-eviction threshold there — stale probes are removed before the forced cap can evict a real session in their place.

Hardening:

- `isGatewayModelRunSessionKey` matches only strict canonical `agent:<id>:explicit:model-run-<uuid>` keys, rejecting unscoped / empty-agent / extra-segment / whitespace keys; normal direct, group, thread, cron, hook, heartbeat, ACP, and sub-agent sessions are never eligible.
- An invalid explicit `modelRunPruneAfter` value fails closed (no pruning) instead of silently enabling the 24h default; the strict session schema rejects invalid explicit values.

`modelRunPruned` / `prune-model-run` are reported separately in cleanup summaries and dry-run plans.

Fixes #88632

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: stale gateway `model-run-<uuid>` probe sessions are pruned before the `maxEntries` cap can evict real sessions, but only under cap pressure when `modelRunPruneAfter` is unset, while explicit config prunes unconditionally and healthy stores are left untouched.
- Real environment tested: local macOS checkout at head `0002eb0770e80c257ae57905ea60487c24a9cd6a`, Node 26.0.0, built CLI via `node scripts/build-all.mjs cliStartup`, isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME` / `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` / `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` and a temp session store under `/tmp/openclaw-91057-proof`.
- Store fixture (re-created before each run): one stale model-run probe (`...174001`, updated 2 days ago), one recent model-run probe (`...174002`, updated now), one durable control (`agent:main:explicit:durable-control`).
- Exact command per scenario:

```console
$ OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-91057-proof/home \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-91057-proof/state \
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<scenario config> \
  node openclaw.mjs sessions cleanup --enforce --json \
  --store /tmp/openclaw-91057-proof/sessions.json
```

- Evidence after fix (copied from the real built CLI runs):

**A — unset default, `maxEntries: 500` (no cap pressure):**

```json
{ "beforeCount": 3, "afterCount": 3, "modelRunPruned": 0, "pruned": 0, "capped": 0 }
```

remaining keys: stale probe, recent probe, durable control all retained — the pressure-gated default does not delete probe-shaped rows from a healthy store.

**B — unset default, `maxEntries: 2` (cap pressure):**

```json
{ "beforeCount": 3, "afterCount": 2, "modelRunPruned": 1, "pruned": 0, "capped": 0 }
```

remaining keys: recent probe + durable control. The stale probe was removed via model-run pruning (`modelRunPruned: 1`) **before** the cap (`capped: 0`), so the durable control survived instead of being evicted.

**C — explicit `modelRunPruneAfter: "24h"`, `maxEntries: 500` (no pressure):**

```json
{ "beforeCount": 3, "afterCount": 2, "modelRunPruned": 1, "pruned": 0, "capped": 0 }
```

remaining keys: recent probe + durable control. Explicit config prunes the stale probe unconditionally even with no cap pressure.

- Observed result: default behavior is pressure-gated (A vs B), explicit config is unconditional (C), the strict probe predicate left the durable control and the recent probe untouched in every case, and under pressure the stale probe is removed by model-run pruning rather than by the cap evicting a real session.
- What was not tested: no live remote gateway model inference call was required; the behavior was exercised through the real built OpenClaw CLI against an isolated session store. No channel, credential, model provider, or gateway transport behavior is changed.

## Testing

- `node scripts/build-all.mjs cliStartup`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.runtime-config.config.ts src/config/sessions/store.pruning.test.ts src/config/sessions/store.pruning.integration.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.session-maintenance-extensions.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts src/config/schema.base.generated.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts src/commands/sessions-cleanup.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/session-store-runtime.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` on touched files

## Current status (2026-06-24)

Default model-run pruning is now **pressure-gated when unset** (Option C) instead of unconditionally on after upgrade: it removes strict `model-run-<uuid>` probe rows only when they would contribute to global cap pressure, so existing healthy stores are not eagerly mutated on upgrade. Explicit `modelRunPruneAfter` opts into unconditional age cleanup; `false` disables.

This directly addresses the earlier `compatibility` / `session-state` merge-risk concerns: the only default-on behavior is removing strict ephemeral probe rows at the same point the cap would otherwise evict real sessions. A self-review pass also fixed a forced-cleanup edge where `sessions cleanup` capped immediately while model-run pruning was still high-water gated, which could let stale probes survive while real sessions were capped — model-run pruning is now aligned to the immediate-cap threshold on forced paths, with a regression test. `maintainerCanModify` is enabled, so this branch is takeover-ready.
target.author: wangwllu

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
